_comment: Mail-server presets. Select one with MAIL_PROVIDER=<key>. Any explicit IMAP_HOST/IMAP_PORT/SMTP_HOST/SMTP_PORT/SMTP_SECURITY env var overrides the preset. Add your own provider by extending this file.

gmail.label: Gmail / Google Workspace
gmail.setup: Enable 2-Step Verification and create an App Password (Google Account > Security > App passwords). Use that 16-char password as IMAP_PASSWORD. IMAP is enabled by default on modern Gmail.

outlook.label: Outlook.com / Hotmail / Live (personal)
outlook.setup: Microsoft is phasing out basic auth for personal accounts; password login may be rejected in favour of OAuth. If it fails, prefer a provider that supports app passwords, or use IMAP OAuth (not covered by this tool).

office365.label: Microsoft 365 (work / school)
office365.setup: Basic auth is often disabled by the tenant admin. You may need the admin to allow IMAP/SMTP AUTH, or use a mailbox that permits app passwords.

icloud.label: iCloud Mail
icloud.setup: Requires an app-specific password (appleid.apple.com > Sign-In and Security > App-Specific Passwords). Use your full @icloud.com address as IMAP_USER.

yahoo.label: Yahoo Mail
yahoo.setup: Create an app password (Yahoo Account Security > Generate app password) and use it as IMAP_PASSWORD.

fastmail.label: Fastmail
fastmail.setup: Create an app password (Settings > Privacy & Security > App passwords) scoped to IMAP+SMTP.

gmx.label: GMX
gmx.setup: Enable IMAP/POP access in GMX settings, then use your account password (or an app password if you have 2FA).

zoho.label: Zoho Mail
zoho.setup: Enable IMAP access in Zoho settings and, if 2FA is on, generate an application-specific password.

opensrs.label: OpenSRS / Tucows Hosted Email (hostedemail.com)
opensrs.setup: Use your full email address as IMAP_USER and your mailbox password. Many resellers brand this service under their own domain.
